description: Islamist Palestinian paramilitary organization formed in 1981 as an offshoot of the Muslim Brotherhood. It seeks the establishment of an Islamic Palestinian state through armed struggle and rejects the Oslo Accords. Its armed wing, Al-Quds Brigades, operates in the West Bank and Gaza Strip. Designated a terrorist organization by several countries.

country_links.evidence_quote: Based in Egypt , Shaqaqi and Awda were originally members of the Muslim Brotherhood ... and conducted operations out of Egypt.
country_links.context: PIJ's founders were based in Egypt and conducted operations out of Egypt before being expelled.

country_links.evidence_quote: Since 2014, PIJ has seen its power steadily increase with the backing of funds from Iran.
country_links.context: PIJ receives funds from Iran and has offices in Tehran.

country_links.evidence_quote: PIJ masterminded several suicide attacks in Israel
country_links.context: PIJ has carried out numerous attacks in Israel, including suicide bombings and rocket fire.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 1988, its leaders were exiled by Israel to Lebanon . While in Lebanon, the group received training, support and other backing from Hezbollah and its backers in Iran
country_links.context: PIJ leaders were exiled to Lebanon where the group received training and support from Hezbollah, and it has offices in Beirut.

country_links.evidence_quote: with offices in Beirut , Tehran , and Khartoum .
country_links.context: PIJ has an office in Khartoum.

country_links.evidence_quote: the headquarters of PIJ moved to the Syrian capital, Damascus, where it continues to be based
country_links.context: PIJ's headquarters moved to Damascus, Syria, where it continues to be based.
